candidates.collision_reason: Paper-like hits are the SIMBAL source atom (arxiv 2506.14038, 'Load Balancing Mixture of Experts with Similarity Preserving Routers') and adjacent MoE-routing works (Self-Routing 2604.00421, ERMoE 2511.10971, Spectral Manifold Regularization 2601.03889, generic orthogonality-regularization papers). None describe the fused niche of similarity-preserving orthogonal routing governing which fast-weight expert undergoes self-supervised test-time training per token. No same-core-niche collision.

candidates.niche_name: Rupture Dynamics of Test-Time-Training Hidden States
candidates.collision_reason: Results split into two disjoint clusters with NO bridging paper: pure seismology (off-fault damage source atom arxiv 1901.01771, 2503.21260, 1901.01219) and pure ML (TTT/representation-geometry: 2603.06642, 2509.23024, 2411.07279). The only neural+seismic hit (RNN for structural damage assessment under seismic loads, PMC12594378) applies ML TO earthquake engineering and does NOT transfer rupture-dynamics physics to explain TTT hidden-state adaptation. No paper occupies the fused niche of modeling TTT hidden-state adaptation as a rupture/off-fault-damage front. No collision; cross-domain prior-art density is low.

candidates.niche_name: Damage-Regularized Orthogonal Routing for Stable MoE Load Balancing
candidates.collision_reason: No paper uses seismic off-fault-damage / rupture as a metaphor for MoE routing stability. ML-side hits are the SIMBAL source atom (2506.14038) plus adjacent MoE-routing/stability works (Spectral Manifold Regularization 2601.03889, bifurcation model 2605.29121, geometric-coupling 2605.12476, z-loss). Seismology-side hits are pure off-fault-damage physics (1901.01771, 2503.21260, 2406.18408). The clusters never merge; the 'damage-regularized routing manifold' framing is the candidate's own and is not occupied in literature. No collision; cross-domain prior-art density is low.
